phase_note: Phase 1 is intentionally weighted toward const and immutability diagnostics; explicit cast edge cases remain inventoried here for follow-up expansion.

cases.upstream_focus: Assignment diagnostics must distinguish illegal targets and immutable targets clearly at compile time.
cases.zr_decision: ZR rejects const reassignment during compilation instead of allowing the write and failing later at runtime.

cases.upstream_focus: Immutable locals need a dedicated reassign diagnostic, not a generic type or parse error.
cases.zr_decision: ZR const locals should emit a focused compile-time failure when assigned after initialization.

cases.upstream_focus: Writes through constant bindings must be rejected regardless of surrounding expression context.
cases.zr_decision: ZR const protection applies to both local and module-visible bindings once the compiler marks them immutable.

cases.upstream_focus: Final or immutable storage rules belong in compile-time diagnostics, not runtime contract checks.
cases.zr_decision: ZR uses compile-time const enforcement as the primary contract for immutable storage.

cases.upstream_focus: Constructor-time immutable storage must be proven assigned on every control-flow path, not just one side of a branch.
cases.zr_decision: ZR rejects const fields left uninitialized after one-armed if or incomplete else-if chains.

cases.upstream_focus: Conditional expressions must merge definite-assignment state from both branches instead of evaluating them linearly.
cases.zr_decision: ZR ternary expressions in constructors now participate in the same const definite-assignment model as statement-level branches.

cases.upstream_focus: Switch statements need conservative definite-assignment merging across cases and unmatched/default paths.
cases.zr_decision: ZR requires constructor const fields to be initialized across every continuing switch path; missing default keeps the field non-definite.

cases.upstream_focus: Immutability must cover parameter bindings as well as standalone variable declarations when the language marks them readonly.
cases.zr_decision: ZR future const-parameter work should reuse the same immutable-assignment diagnostic model as const locals.

cases.upstream_focus: Const-like type metadata at the ABI or signature layer should stay separate from ordinary assignment semantics but remain traceable.
cases.zr_decision: ZR records const and explicit-cast precedent in one matrix, while phase 1 only turns immutable assignment into a runnable fixture.

cases.upstream_focus: Repository-local fixture proving constructor const definite assignment across nested else-if chains.
cases.zr_decision: The reference fixture suite includes a compile-success baseline for else-if coverage.

cases.upstream_focus: Repository-local fixture proving a one-armed if leaves constructor const fields uninitialized on continuing paths.
cases.zr_decision: The reference fixture suite includes a compile-fail baseline for incomplete if-path coverage.

cases.upstream_focus: Repository-local fixture proving constructor const definite assignment across switch cases plus default.
cases.zr_decision: The reference fixture suite includes a compile-success baseline for switch path coverage.

cases.upstream_focus: Repository-local fixture proving missing default leaves unmatched switch paths uninitialized.
cases.zr_decision: The reference fixture suite includes a compile-fail baseline for incomplete switch path coverage.

cases.upstream_focus: Repository-local fixture proving ternary branches merge const definite-assignment state by intersection.
cases.zr_decision: The reference fixture suite includes a compile-success baseline for ternary branch coverage.

cases.upstream_focus: Repository-local fixture proving ternary branches that initialize only one side are rejected.
cases.zr_decision: The reference fixture suite includes a compile-fail baseline for incomplete ternary coverage.
